name: Vest-Agder (Norway, 1958–2019)

colors.symbolism: the field of the county arms; the forests of Vest-Agder

description: A green field charged with a stylised gold oak.
history: Granted to Vest-Agder county on 14 February 1958. The county was abolished on 1 January 2020 when it was merged with Aust-Agder into the new Agder county.

features.symbolism: heraldic banner of the county arms: a gold oak on a green field, evoking the rich oak forests of Vest-Agder
the oak forests historically important to Vest-Agder's economy and shipbuilding
